skill.name: vercel-deploy
skill.description: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel instantly. Use when asked to "Deploy my app", "Deploy this to production", "Create a preview deployment", or "Push this live". No authentication required - returns preview URL and claimable deployment link.
skill.summary: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel instantly with automatic framework detection and claimable deployments.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 60 potential issues (external_commands, network, filesystem), but manual review confirms all are false positives or legitimate deployment operations. Shell commands use hardcoded arguments, network calls target official Vercel endpoints, and temp files are properly cleaned up. This is a standard deployment automation skill with no security concerns.

content.value_statement: Deploy web applications to Vercel instantly without authentication. Automatically detect frameworks and return both preview and claim URLs.
actual_capabilities: Deploy any web application to Vercel with automatic framework detection
Package projects as tarballs excluding node_modules and .git directories
Return both Preview URL (live site) and Claim URL (account transfer)
Handle static HTML projects with automatic index.html detection
Support 30+ frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, Astro, Express
limitations: Requires network access to reach Vercel deployment API
Cannot deploy without a project directory or tarball
Framework detection requires package.json for most frameworks
Claim URL transfer must be completed by user in Vercel dashboard

use_cases.title: Quick App Deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy a web application to a live URL instantly for testing or sharing
use_cases.target_user: Developers who need to quickly share their work

use_cases.title: Preview Environments
use_cases.description: Create preview deployments for pull requests or feature branches

use_cases.title: Static Site Hosting
use_cases.description: Host static HTML sites or documentation without configuration

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Current Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my current project to Vercel
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to deploy the project in the current directory

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Specific Path
prompt_templates.scenario: When user specifies a specific project path

prompt_templates.title: Deploy and Share Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this to production and give me the link
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants deployment with shareable URLs

prompt_templates.title: Create Preview Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a preview deployment for review
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs a temporary URL for review purposes

View your site at the Preview URL.
To transfer this deployment to your Vercel account, visit the Claim URL.

Creating deployment package...
Deploying...
✓ Deployment successful!

Preview URL: https://skill-deploy-def456.vercel.app
Claim URL: https://vercel.com/claim-deployment?code=...

best_practices: Always show both Preview URL and Claim URL to the user
Verify deployment success before presenting results
Handle network errors with clear troubleshooting steps
anti_patterns: Deploying without verifying the project structure first
Only showing one URL when both are available
Hardcoding credentials when the claimable flow requires none

faq.question: Do I need Vercel authentication?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses a claimable deployment flow. The Preview URL works immediately, and the Claim URL lets you transfer ownership to your Vercel account.

faq.question: What frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Supports 30+ frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, Astro, Angular, Express, Fastify, and more. Auto-detected from package.json.

faq.question: Can I deploy a static HTML site?
faq.answer: Yes. If no package.json exists, the skill detects HTML files and will rename a single HTML file to index.html for root URL access.

faq.question: What is the Claim URL for?
faq.answer: The Claim URL lets you transfer the deployment to your Vercel account. Without claiming, the deployment remains tied to the deployment service.

faq.question: Why did deployment fail?
faq.answer: Common reasons: network restrictions blocking vercel.com, missing package.json, or invalid project structure. Check the error message for details.

faq.question: Are node_modules included in deployment?
faq.answer: No. The tarball explicitly excludes node_modules and .git directories for faster uploads and smaller package size.
